title: Zephyranthes aurata
taxonomy: Zephyranthes aurata is classified within the family Amaryllidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. This family is part of the order Asparagales, which also includes various other plant families such as the orchids and the lilies. At the kingdom level, Zephyranthes aurata falls under Plantae, which encompasses all types of flowering and non-flowering plants. The kingdom Plantae is further divided into several other taxonomic ranks, including the division Magnoliophyta, which contains the flowering plants. Overall, the classification of Zephyranthes aurata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species within the Amaryllidaceae family.

intro: Zephyranthes aurata, commonly known as the Golden Zephyr or Golden Rain Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes aurata include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which have been identified as responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tea infusions, capsules, and topical creams, which are used to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
